title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's New Default Model
summary: OpenAI has replaced its default ChatGPT model with GPT-5.5 Instant, featuring improved factual accuracy, more concise responses, and enhanced personalization for paid users.
content: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default model for ChatGPT, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant with a focus on reducing hallucinations—particularly in sensitive domains like law, medicine, and finance. The model scored 81.2 on the AIME 2025 mathematics benchmark, up from 65.4 for its predecessor, and delivers tighter, more direct answers described by OpenAI as less verbose and free of "overformatting and gratuitous emojis." For Plus and Pro subscribers, GPT-5.5 Instant now leverages past conversations, uploaded files, and connected Gmail inboxes to generate more personalized responses. OpenAI is also rolling out "memory sources" to all ChatGPT users, showing exactly what information the chatbot used to personalize a response, with options to delete or correct those memories. The old GPT-5.3 model will remain available for three months via the model picker for paid users before retirement.

title: OpenAI Introduces Next-Generation Realtime Voice Models with GPT-5-Class Reasoning
summary: OpenAI launched three new realtime audio models—GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper—bringing GPT-5-level intelligence and live multilingual translation to voice applications.
content: OpenAI unveiled three new models in its Realtime API designed to move voice AI from simple call-and-response toward interfaces that can listen, reason, translate, and take action in real time. GPT-Realtime-2 is the first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning, supporting a 128K context window, parallel tool calls, adjustable reasoning effort, and a 15.2% improvement on audio intelligence benchmarks over its predecessor—with Zillow reporting a 26-point lift in call success rate on its hardest adversarial benchmark. GPT-Realtime-Translate handles live speech translation across 70+ input languages into 13 output languages, enabling multilingual customer support without separate localization workflows. GPT-Realtime-Whisper provides streaming low-latency speech-to-text that transcribes audio as people speak, suitable for live captions, real-time meeting notes, and high-volume customer support. Early production partners include Zillow, Deutsche Telekom, Priceline, and BolnaAI, which reported 12.5% lower word error rates across Hindi, Tamil, and Telugu compared to any other model tested.

title: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve Moves from Pilot to Core Infrastructure Component
summary: Google DeepMind announced that AlphaEvolve, its Gemini-powered algorithm discovery agent, has graduated from research pilot to a production component of Google's infrastructure and is now expanding to commercial customers globally.
content: AlphaEvolve, Google DeepMind's Gemini-powered coding agent that autonomously discovers and optimizes algorithms, has been promoted from a research pilot to a core infrastructure tool running across Google. The system designed a circuit for Google's next-generation TPUs so counterintuitive yet efficient that it was integrated directly into the chip's silicon, and separately helped reduce write amplification in Google Spanner's storage engine by 20% while cutting software storage footprints by nearly 9%. In genomics, AlphaEvolve improved DeepConsensus's DNA sequencing error correction by 30%, and in grid optimization it increased feasible solution rates for the AC Optimal Power Flow problem from 14% to over 88%. Externally, commercial partners including Klarna, Schrödinger, and FM Logistic reported results such as doubling transformer training speed, 4x inference speedups in drug discovery workflows, and 10.4% routing efficiency improvements saving over 15,000 km of distance annually. AlphaEvolve has also collaborated with mathematician Terence Tao on open problems in combinatorics and broken records for the Traveling Salesman Problem and Ramsey Numbers.

title: Anthropic and SpaceX Partner to Unlock 300+ Megawatts of AI Compute at Colossus 1
summary: Anthropic has signed a deal to access all compute capacity at SpaceX's Colossus 1 data center in Memphis, adding over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs to power Claude's rapidly growing user base.
content: Anthropic announced a major compute partnership with SpaceX, gaining access to the full capacity of Colossus 1—SpaceX's AI data center in Memphis, Tennessee—providing more than 300 megawatts of power and over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs within a month. The deal directly addresses surging demand for Claude products, including the Claude Code AI coding agent, and will allow Anthropic to immediately double Claude Code's five-hour usage limits for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans while eliminating peak-hour capacity reductions. At a developer day held concurrently in San Francisco, Anthropic unveiled a new "dreaming" feature that lets Claude agents autonomously review their own work between sessions, identify patterns, and update context files—a self-improvement capability aimed at enterprise customers. The company also revealed ten new financially focused AI agents and disclosed that the technology sector is now its largest source of enterprise revenue, followed by financial institutions. Anthropic additionally expressed interest in partnering with SpaceX to develop multiple gigawatts of orbital, space-based AI compute—one of Elon Musk's long-term goals and a key driver behind SpaceX's anticipated IPO.

title: All Five Major US AI Labs Now Submit Models to Government for Pre-Release Security Review
summary: Google, Microsoft, and xAI joined OpenAI and Anthropic in voluntarily giving the US Commerce Department's CAISI pre-release access to evaluate frontier AI models, covering all major US labs for the first time.
content: The US Commerce Department announced that Google, Microsoft, and xAI have joined OpenAI and Anthropic in giving the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) pre-release access to their frontier AI models for national security evaluation—a first for comprehensive voluntary coverage of the US AI industry. The expansion was triggered by Anthropic's Mythos model, which can autonomously discover and exploit zero-day vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers and prompted alarm from the White House, the NSA, and foreign governments. The voluntary agreements allow CAISI to probe models for dangerous capabilities including cyberattack automation, biological weapon synthesis pathways, and uncontrollable autonomous agent behaviors, but the office's fewer than 200 staff have no statutory authority to delay or block a release. The Trump administration—which had previously opposed Biden-era AI oversight efforts—is now reportedly drafting an executive order that would formalize the review process and could grant the government mandatory pre-deployment approval authority over frontier models. Analysts note the system's structural fragility: it is entirely dependent on voluntary industry cooperation, and any company could legally withdraw and release a model without government review.

title: EU Strikes Deal to Simplify AI Act Compliance and Ban Non-Consensual AI Imagery
summary: European Parliament and Council negotiators reached a provisional agreement to delay and streamline AI Act obligations for businesses while introducing an immediate ban on AI-generated non-consensual intimate imagery.
content: European Union co-legislators reached a provisional deal to amend the EU AI Act, pushing back and simplifying compliance deadlines for businesses while adding new prohibitions on the most harmful AI applications. Under the agreement, high-risk AI systems—including those used in biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, law enforcement, and border management—will not face compliance obligations until December 2027, with products covered by EU safety legislation given until August 2028. The deal bans AI systems capable of generating non-consensual intimate images or child sexual abuse material, requiring companies to remove such capabilities by December 2026, and also delays watermarking obligations on AI-generated content to December 2026. To reduce regulatory burden, overlapping requirements between the AI Act and EU machinery safety legislation have been eliminated, and SME exemptions have been extended to small mid-cap enterprises to support their growth. The provisional agreement is part of the EU's broader Digital Omnibus simplification package and must be formally adopted by both Parliament and Council before August 2026, when the current high-risk rules were originally set to take effect.
